req.title: Audit log access
req.statement: A privacy-officer shall access audit logs scoped to their organisation; cross-organisation access shall be forbidden.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-014 mandates role-based access control (RBAC) for audit log retrieval, scoped by organisation. Privacy officers must retrieve only logs within their assigned organisation; cross-organisation queries must be rejected with 403 Forbidden. Test design applies Equivalence Partitioning (same-org vs cross-org query classes), Boundary Value Analysis (org boundary transitions), Negative Path (403 error handling), and Decision Table (role + org combination logic). Three test cases cover: (1) positive case—valid same-org query returns 200 with filtered records; (2) negative case—cross-org query returns 403; (3) boundary case—query at exact org boundary returns 403.

tests.name: test_audit_log_same_org_returns_200_with_records
preconditions: User authenticated as privacy-officer with role token
Audit events exist for ORG-A in database
actions: Set Authorization header with valid privacy-officer token for ORG-A
Send GET request to /AuditEvent with query param ?_organization=ORG-A
expected: Response body contains Bundle resource type
All returned AuditEvent entries belong to ORG-A
trace: R-HLT-014: Privacy officer queries logs within assigned org
Expected outcome: records returned within org
tests.executable:
import requests
import json

def test_audit_log_same_org_returns_200_with_records():
    token = <get_privacy_officer_token_for_org>('ORG-A')
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
    url = 'http://localhost:8080/AuditEvent'
    params = {'_organization': 'ORG-A'}
    
    resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers, params=params)
    
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get('resourceType') == 'Bundle'
    assert body.get('total', 0) >= 1
    
    for entry in body.get('entry', []):
        org = entry['resource'].get('agent', [{}])[0].get('organization')
        assert org == 'ORG-A' or 'ORG-A' in str(entry)

tests.name: test_audit_log_cross_org_query_returns_403
preconditions: User authenticated as privacy-officer with role token
Audit events exist for ORG-B in database
ORG-B is different from user's assigned org
actions: Set Authorization header with valid privacy-officer token for ORG-A
Send GET request to /AuditEvent with query param ?_organization=ORG-B
Capture response status and error detail
expected: Response body contains error detail or OperationOutcome
trace: R-HLT-014: Privacy officer attempts cross-organisation access
Error pathway: 403 when crossing org boundary
Token scope: ORG-A; Query scope: ORG-B
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_audit_log_cross_org_query_returns_403():
    token = <get_privacy_officer_token_for_org>('ORG-A')
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
    url = 'http://localhost:8080/AuditEvent'
    params = {'_organization': 'ORG-B'}
    
    resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers, params=params)
    
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert 'error' in body or body.get('resourceType') == 'OperationOutcome'
    assert body.get('entry') is None or len(body.get('entry', [])) == 0

tests.name: test_audit_log_boundary_org_transition_returns_403
preconditions: User authenticated as privacy-officer for ORG-A
Adjacent organisation ORG-A-1 exists (similar ID prefix, distinct entity)
actions: Set Authorization header with token scoped to ORG-A
Verify boundary rejection at org ID boundary
expected: No data leakage across similar org IDs
trace: R-HLT-014: Boundary case—similar org ID at exact scope boundary
Heuristic: BVA on org identifier string boundary
Precondition: role:privacy-officer with org scope ORG-A

tests.name: test_audit_log_missing_org_param_uses_token_scope
preconditions: User authenticated as privacy-officer for ORG-A
Audit events exist for multiple organisations
Server implements implicit org scoping from token
actions: Set Authorization header with token for ORG-A
Send GET request to /AuditEvent without _organization param
expected: Returned records filtered to ORG-A only
trace: R-HLT-014: Decision table—query param omitted, token scope applied
Heuristic: DT combining (param presence, token scope) → outcome
Ensures org boundary enforced even without explicit param
